sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Prime vs. Tier 2 vs. New Entrant Positioning
required_elements: Defense industrial base hierarchy classification (prime, tier 2/3, new entrant)
Revenue composition by customer (DoD, international FMS/DCS, commercial/adjacent)
Program concentration risk quantification (top 5 programs as % of revenue)
Board connectivity to defense appropriators and acquisition officials assessment

sections.title: Backlog & Pipeline Economics — The Revenue Visibility Framework
required_elements: Total backlog decomposition (funded, unfunded, total potential) and book-to-bill ratio
Backlog duration (funded backlog / trailing revenue = years of visibility)
Contract mix breakdown (cost-plus-fee, fixed-price-incentive, firm-fixed-price margins)
Award pipeline mapping against DoD FYDP and congressional marks

sections.title: Geopolitical Demand Drivers & Budget Cycle Analysis
required_elements: Macro defense spending trajectory (U.S. DoD base + OCO, NATO 2% compliance, Indo-Pacific deterrence)
Threat vector alignment analysis (China/Russia peer competition, counter-UAS, cyber, space)
Budget cycle risk scenarios (continuing resolution, sequestration, Budget Control Act)
International market dynamics (FMS pipeline, offset requirements, technology transfer restrictions)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Operating margin bridge (current → target, drivers identified)
Contract mix shift analysis (cost-plus to fixed-price margin expansion pathway)
Production rate increase and operational efficiency impact on margins
Pension-related distortions and R&D accounting normalization

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Fixed-price program margin compression (cost overrun indicator)
EAC adjustments reducing backlog profitability
Rising contract protest rates (competitive intensity signal)
Bookings growth vs. revenue growth divergence (backlog conversion delays)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Program-of-record risk assessment for top 5 revenue contributors
FMS pipeline deep dive for 3 largest international opportunities
Workforce and supply chain bottleneck analysis (skilled labor, specialty metals)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Backlog visibility explicitly calculated (funded backlog / revenue = years)
Contract mix breakdown with margin percentages for each contract type
Program concentration risk quantified (single program max % of revenue)
DoD budget scenario modeling present (continuing resolution impact, sequestration risk)
Margin bridge analysis showing path from current to target operating margin
Investigation tracks include specific programs, international markets, and supply chain constraints
